review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Button-group abstraction passes a very wide prop bag with almost no behavior

evidence: UseButtonGroupPropsParams collects a large mixed option bag, and useButtonGroupProps mostly re-emits those fields into topLeft/topRight/bottomLeft/bottomRight.
useSharedButtonGroupState in useSharedLightboxState extracts values and forwards them almost unchanged into useButtonGroupProps.
The only added logic is thin wrappers (for delete/upscale), so the layer adds ceremony and maintenance surface more than design leverage.
detail.suggestion: Replace this with narrower per-group builders (or a compact LightboxActions model) so each control group receives only the fields it actually uses.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Inline edit state hook adds a large DTO layer used by only one consumer

evidence: useInlineEditState defines multiple internal DTO interfaces (CanvasEnvironmentState, InpaintingState, TransformState, GenerationState) that are only used in this module.
buildImageEditValueParams and buildInlineEditStateResult mostly map existing hook outputs into new shapes with 1:1 property forwarding.
useInlineEditState is consumed directly by InlineEditView, so these extra DTO assembly layers do not provide polymorphic reuse.
detail.suggestion: Collapse the intermediate DTO builders and expose a smaller set of focused sub-hooks (or return existing hook results directly) to reduce forwarding-only abstraction.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Lightbox state context hook is mostly a one-to-one translation layer

evidence: UseLightboxStateValueInput is a very wide interface, but useLightboxStateValue mainly rebuilds objects with direct field assignment.
Most transformations are naming wrappers (set* to handle*) and async pass-through wrappers that do not change behavior.
Both Image and Video render paths already prepare the same data, so this hook introduces an extra translation boundary with little policy.
detail.suggestion: Pass already-grouped submodels (core/media/variants/navigation) directly to the provider, and remove the translation layer unless it enforces real cross-cutting behavior.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Timestamp hooks duplicate the same live-date abstraction and keep a no-op alias

evidence: useCompletedTimestamp and useProcessingTimestamp each define a local useLiveTimestamp with the same parsing and updater mechanics.
The two hooks differ primarily by formatter string, but maintain separate internal abstraction stacks.
useTaskTimestamp forwards directly to useUpdatingTimestamp with the same default abbreviation behavior, adding an extra named layer without new policy.
detail.suggestion: Extract one shared relative-timestamp hook with formatter input, and either remove useTaskTimestamp or make it enforce task-specific behavior that differs from defaults.

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Barrel modules carry multi-line commentary for near-trivial re-exports.

evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useShots.ts` uses a 6-line explanatory header above a single `export * from './shots'`.
`src/types/shots.ts` has a multi-line deprecation block above one re-export.
`src/shared/components/SegmentSettingsForm/components/index.ts` adds a docblock for only two obvious exports.
detail.suggestion: Trim barrel-file comments to one concise compatibility note only where needed, and move migration/background details to a dedicated migration doc.

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Several helper files use repetitive docstrings that mostly restate signatures.

evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/errorUtils.ts` repeats one-line purpose comments above tiny guards like `isError`, `isErrorWithCode`, and `isErrorWithStatus`.
`src/shared/hooks/useProcessingTimestamp.ts` includes extensive explanatory prose and example usage around a thin wrapper that delegates to `useLiveTimestamp`.
detail.suggestion: Collapse repeated prose into a short module-level contract and keep per-function comments only for non-obvious behavior (e.g., DOMException edge cases).

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Aspect-ratio parsing/fallback logic is duplicated across utilities.

evidence: `getAspectRatioStyle` in `src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts` parses `w:h` and falls back to `'1'`.
`getImageAspectRatioStyle` in `src/shared/lib/imageAspectRatio.ts` duplicates the same parse/`isNaN`/fallback pattern for project ratio handling.
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared `parseAspectRatio` helper and reuse it in both modules to remove copy-paste formulaic logic.

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Debug polling methods repeat the same try/catch/log/boolean template.

evidence: `testConnection` and `testTaskStatusQuery` both implement nearly identical flow: run query, log error, return false, catch/log, return false.
The repeated error-handling template creates boilerplate-heavy debugging code with little per-method differentiation.
detail.suggestion: Add a shared `runDebugCheck(label, checkFn)` wrapper for standardized error handling and move method-specific logic to query construction only.

review::.::holistic::contract_coherence:.summary: Auth redirect comment states a different storage-key contract than the code uses.

evidence: The comment says the key is `sb-${hostname[0]}-auth-token`, but implementation derives `projectRef` from `new URL(...).hostname.split('.')[0]`.
This mismatch makes the documented contract incorrect for anyone reusing the logic.
detail.suggestion: Update the comment to match the actual key derivation (`sb-${projectRef}-auth-token`) and preferably reuse a shared helper to avoid future drift.

review::.::holistic::contract_coherence:.summary: Inline edit state exposes mutator APIs that are hardcoded no-ops.

evidence: `useImageEditValue` sets `setIsMagicEditMode`, `setQwenEditModel`, and `setAdvancedSettings` to `() => {}` while still exposing corresponding state fields.
`handleExitInpaintMode` is wired to `handleExitMagicEditMode`, which breaks method-name/behavior alignment in the returned state contract.
`InlineEditView` passes this state directly to `EditModePanel` via `imageEditState={state.imageEditValue}`, so UI controls can invoke mutators that do not actually mutate anything.
detail.suggestion: Either wire these mutators to real state in inline-edit mode, or narrow the inline-edit API by removing/omitting unsupported mutators and disabling corresponding panel controls.

review::.::holistic::contract_coherence:.summary: Session helpers declare string returns but leak unvalidated JSON values.

evidence: `readRawSession` parses arbitrary localStorage JSON into `Record<string, unknown>`, then `readAccessTokenFromStorage` returns `(parsed as { access_token?: string }).access_token ?? null` without a runtime string check.
`readUserIdFromStorage` uses the same unchecked cast pattern for `user.id`, so malformed payloads can violate the `string | null` return contract at runtime.
`hasStoredSession` in `src/app/routes.tsx` also trusts casted `access_token` truthiness, creating another contract path that can disagree with strict string expectations.
detail.suggestion: Add runtime guards (`typeof value === 'string'`) before returning token/user id, and have `hasStoredSession` call a shared validated helper instead of re-parsing with casts.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: GlobalHeader auth path hides query failures as normal empty data

evidence: `fetchUsername` returns `null` for both missing row and query error without error handling (lines 7-18).
Referral stats logic sets `{total_visits: 0, successful_referrals: 0}` for both no-data and error cases (lines 87-98).
These branches erase failure context, making downstream behavior indistinguishable from legitimate empty state.
detail.suggestion: Separate `not_found` and `error` outcomes, and route backend failures through `handleError(..., { showToast: false })`. Keep zero defaults only for true empty-data cases and expose an error state for observability.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: invokeWithTimeout reports every AbortError as timeout, including caller cancellations

evidence: The function composes caller-provided and internal timeout signals (lines 21-31), so both cancel paths surface as AbortError.
The catch block maps any AbortError to `new Error("Function ... timed out")` (lines 62-64), regardless of why abort happened.
This also drops original error cause/context, making cancellation vs timeout hard to trace.
detail.suggestion: Track whether the internal timer fired (for example, a `didTimeout` flag). Only convert AbortError to timeout when that flag is true; otherwise rethrow the original abort (or wrap with `cause`) so callers can distinguish cancellation from timeout.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Project CRUD can return success after default-shot/template setup failures

evidence: `copyTemplateToNewUser` logs/handles errors but never propagates them (lines 53-60).
`createDefaultShot` returns `null` on DB failure (lines 82-95).
Callers in `fetchProjects` and `addNewProject` await `createDefaultShot(...)` but ignore its nullable result (lines 187 and 242), so parent flow can continue as success.
detail.suggestion: Use one explicit contract for setup failures: either throw and fail the parent operation, or return a typed non-fatal result that callers must handle. Do not silently continue when required setup steps fail.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Share page maps RPC/query failures to 'not found' instead of a distinct error path

evidence: The same branch handles `fetchError`, invalid payload shape, and domain payload errors with `setError('Share not found...')` (lines 67-70).
No `handleError` call occurs for `fetchError` in that branch, so operational failures lose context.
This makes user-facing and diagnostic behavior inconsistent across failure modes.
detail.suggestion: Split error handling paths: log/normalize transport or query failures with `handleError`, and reserve the 'not found' message for explicit domain misses.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Variant image loader rethrows DB errors as generic strings without original context

evidence: `loadSingleImage` throws `new Error(...)` from insert/update failures (lines 201-203 and 216-218).
Only `error.message` is preserved; Supabase metadata (code/details/hint/status) is discarded before upper-layer handling.
Upstream handlers receive inconsistent, flattened errors that are harder to classify and debug.
detail.suggestion: Propagate original Supabase errors directly, or wrap with preserved cause/context (`normalizeAppError` or `new Error(msg, { cause: error })`) so structured metadata survives.

review::.::holistic::logic_clarity::add_.summary: Adapter always reports success even when add-without-position returns false.

evidence: `onAddToShotWithoutPosition` is declared as `Promise<boolean>` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:30`.

`handleAddToShotWithoutPositionAdapter` awaits that handler but unconditionally returns `true` on the non-throw path (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:166-173`).
`executeWithRetry` treats the returned boolean as the success gate before triggering optimistic UI updates (`src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useShotActions.ts:77-81`).
detail.suggestion: Return the underlying boolean result instead of forcing success, e.g. `return await onAddToShotWithoutPosition(targetShotId, generationId);` and keep `catch` returning `false`.

review::.::holistic::naming_quality::bui.summary: `buildBasicModePhaseConfig` has conflicting units and return shapes across modules.

evidence: `phaseConfig.ts` exports `buildBasicModePhaseConfig(useVaceModel, _amountOfMotion, userLoras): PhaseConfig` with motion normalized to 0-1.
`generateVideoService.ts` exports another `buildBasicModePhaseConfig(amountOfMotion, userLoras)` that expects 0-100 and returns `{ model, phaseConfig }`.
Same name hides important differences in unit expectations and output contract.
detail.suggestion: Rename the service wrapper to an intent-specific name (for example `buildBasicModeGenerationRequest`) and rename the numeric argument to `motionPercent` to encode units at call sites.

review::.::holistic::naming_quality::cal.summary: `calculateNextAvailableFrame` names a pure helper and an async DB-backed operation.

evidence: `timelinePositionCalculator.ts` exports `calculateNextAvailableFrame(existingFrames, explicitSpacing?)` as a synchronous pure function.
`timelineDropHelpers.ts` exports `calculateNextAvailableFrame(shotId, targetFrame)` as an async Supabase query with error fallbacks.
Both symbols imply the same operation name while one is local math and the other is remote state lookup.
detail.suggestion: Rename the async variant to something side-effect explicit (for example `fetchNextAvailableFrameForShot`) and keep the pure calculator name for local computation only.

review::.::holistic::naming_quality::dup.summary: `deriveInputImages` is exported with different contracts and behaviors.

evidence: `taskParamsUtils.ts` defines `deriveInputImages(parsedParams)` for parsed params with orchestrator/segment fallbacks.
`task-utils.ts` defines `deriveInputImages(task)` and branches on `task.taskType`.
`mediaGallery-utils.ts` defines `deriveInputImages(task)` with quote-cleaning behavior that differs from the other two.
detail.suggestion: Use contract-specific names (for example `deriveInputImagesFromParams`, `deriveInputImagesFromTask`, `deriveInputImagesForGallery`) and centralize shared extraction logic in one canonical helper.

review::.::holistic::naming_quality::med.summary: `mediaGallery-constants.ts` contains layout algorithms, not just constants.

evidence: The file defines behavior-heavy helpers like `calculateDynamicColumns`, `calculateDynamicRows`, and `getLayoutForAspectRatio`.
`getLayoutForAspectRatio` also depends on container dimensions and spacing flags, so the name is narrower than its behavior.
A helper named `getTargetImageWidth(_aspectRatio)` accepts an aspect ratio argument it does not use, which weakens intent signaling.
detail.suggestion: Split constants from computation (`mediaGalleryLayout.constants.ts` + `mediaGalleryLayout.ts`), and rename `getLayoutForAspectRatio` to `calculateGalleryLayout` (or equivalent) to match actual inputs and behavior.

review::.::holistic::type_safety::nullab.summary: `task_id` is nullable at runtime but force-cast to non-null string

evidence: Code comment states generated types mark `task_id` non-null while DB behavior is nullable (`src/shared/hooks/useShareGeneration.ts:316-317`).
Insert payload uses `task_id: (taskId || null) as unknown as string` (`src/shared/hooks/useShareGeneration.ts:322`).
Compile-time contract (`string`) therefore does not match possible runtime value (`null`).
detail.suggestion: Align insert typing with runtime (`task_id: string | null`) by regenerating Supabase types or introducing a local insert payload type, then remove the `unknown` double cast.

review::.::holistic::type_safety::raw_da.summary: Realtime event type encodes DELETE payloads as non-null `new` records

evidence: `RawDatabaseEvent` always declares `new: T` regardless of `eventType` (`src/shared/realtime/types.ts:53-57`).
Processor methods repeatedly cast `e.new` to concrete records to satisfy this contract (`src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:174`, `src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:248`).

Delete handlers already need `(e.old || e.new)` fallbacks, showing runtime payload shape differs from the type (`src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:316-317`, `src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:417`).
detail.suggestion: Model `RawDatabaseEvent` as a discriminated union by `eventType` (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) with DELETE using `old` as the authoritative row and `new` as nullable/partial. Then remove `as unknown as` casts and rely on narrowed types.

review::.::holistic::type_safety::shot_g.summary: Shot-generation DELETE processing reads `e.new` and can emit invalid typed IDs

evidence: All `shot_generations` events are routed to `processShotGenerationChanges`, including DELETE (`src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:150-151`).
That method always reads `const newRecord = e.new as ...` and then uses `newRecord.shot_id`/`newRecord.generation_id` (`src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:344-360`).
Unlike generation/variant delete handlers, there is no `e.old` fallback for DELETE rows (`src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:316-317`, `src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:417`).
detail.suggestion: Handle DELETE explicitly in `processShotGenerationChanges`: read from `(e.old ?? e.new)`, validate required IDs, and skip/guard malformed payloads before building `ShotGenerationsChangedEvent`.

review::.::holistic::type_safety::task_p.summary: Task param helpers cast unknown JSON to `string`/`string[]` without narrowing

evidence: `deriveInputImages` returns `(Array.isArray(images) ? images : []).filter(Boolean) as string[]`, which does not verify element types (`src/shared/components/TasksPane/utils/task-utils.ts:22`).
Several paths spread arrays cast as `string[]` from unknown JSON (`src/shared/components/TasksPane/utils/task-utils.ts:30-44`).

Extractor functions cast nested unknown fields directly to `string` in OR chains (`src/shared/components/TasksPane/utils/task-utils.ts:81-84`, `src/shared/components/TasksPane/utils/task-utils.ts:96-100`, `src/shared/components/TasksPane/utils/task-utils.ts:112-114`).
detail.suggestion: Introduce runtime guards (`getString`, `getStringArray`) that validate `typeof value === 'string'` per element before returning typed values; otherwise return `null`/`undefined` safely.

review::.::holistic::type_safety::varian.summary: Variant conversion returns untyped records and is force-cast to `GenerationRow`

evidence: `variantToGenerationRow` returns `Record<string, unknown>` instead of a concrete row type (`src/shared/lib/mediaTypeHelpers.ts:67-79`).
It sets `id` from `getGenerationId`, whose contract is `string | null` (`src/shared/lib/mediaTypeHelpers.ts:22-30`, `src/shared/lib/mediaTypeHelpers.ts:81`).
Callers silence mismatch via `as unknown as GenerationRow` (`src/tools/edit-video/pages/EditVideoPage.tsx:120`, `src/tools/edit-images/pages/EditImagesPage.tsx:210`).
detail.suggestion: Return a strict type from `variantToGenerationRow` (e.g., `GenerationRow` or a dedicated lightbox row), enforce/derive non-null `id` inside the helper, and remove call-site double assertions.
